content.thinking:
The diff looks clean. The only changes are to the shader file - converting `constant half*` to `device const half*` and adding TG memory caching for both the `q4mv_spec_f16io_resadd` and `q4mv_spec_f16in` kernels. No Zig dispatch changes needed.

Results are consistent: 220.0-220.2 tok/s, up from 214-218 baseline. Let me keep this.

content.text: The diff is clean - only shader changes, no Zig dispatch changes needed. Consistent ~220 tok/s improvement. Let me keep it.

input.summary: Switched q4mv_spec_f16io_resadd (O proj, 28× per token) and q4mv_spec_f16in (LM head, 1× per token) from `constant half*` input to `device const half*` with cooperative threadgroup memory caching. The interleaved SIMD access pattern means different lanes read different input addresses, defeating the constant cache's broadcast optimization. TG memory (on-chip SRAM) provides single-cycle access for non-uniform reads. Each TG cooperatively loads K halfs (4KB for K=2048) from device memory, then all SIMD groups read from fast TG memory. Result: 220.0-220.2 tok/s (up from 214-218 baseline), consistent across 3 runs. Prefill also improved to ~105 tok/s. p99 latency stable at ~4750μs.
